other ideas: pond sweep me off my feet
norah jones the nearness of you

dailysoundtracks.description: Tori sings about how she wants to kill this bitch but she believes in peace and will control her impulses. In this performance she goes off on a massive ad-lib and it's just so great.

dailysoundtracks.artist: King Gizzard And The Lizard Wizard
dailysoundtracks.description: This song has so much going on. The little microtonal riff at the start sounds like they're going for ancient Egypt, but then there's this bluegrass break. It's a lot of fun!
Beautiful, peaceful album finisher. Great for when you're at the acceptance stage of your grief.
Very memorable and impactful music from the game's first boss. The whole soundtrack has this gritty saw-wave sound. It just sounds so devastating.
Such a fun disco track with Chinese vibes. Love DJing this one.
Banger western desert disco level music. So addicting.
This is an insane masterpiece from Björk which sounds like it could be in a Bond film. So intense and so good.
It's a pretty out-there chill track. Hard to pin a genre on this one, but it's a vibe.
An intense sexy song. 'I wanna make love to you darling, and deal with the mess we made in the morning'. This one is FIRMLY on the babymaking playlist.
Had a moment with this song.
Love the beginning of this song with the piano, and then it just turns into this super cool western-style bop.
Some fun rock music from the 80s with some sensitive songwriting.
This song inspired me to make a playlist of songs which evoke the feeling of the wind blowing through your hair. The part where the tempo slows down? Sublime.
Such a feel-good gem that I found thanks to the algorithm many years ago.
Lianne coming in and just sounding so gorgeous on this track... stunning.
She covers Radiohead for this one and it's so great. It's how I got to know about the Radiohead song if I'm honest.
Such an intense vibe for such a slow song. Love it.
This song is so fun! I didn't know about it back then but Shamir is a queer icon. I like how the synth sounds like Mega Bomberman.
Fast-paced but still chill music. Might be good if you're taking uppers at home
I know I know it's not cool to like Coldplay songs but for whatever reason I really like this one. I like it when he says 'of course I do'

dailysoundtracks.album: The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess
dailysoundtracks.description: I slept on this originally but now I recognise it for one of the best little town tunes in Zelda history. It's so chill and I love it.
Wow I didn't know about this music video until just now. Yes, this song sounds exactly like swarms of insects. It's creepy and amazing.
Some 60's music. Love how the melody stays the same over the changing harmony.
Erykah released this one off track and I just love it. Love the guitar twang and the chord sequence.
Devastating song about unrequited love
This is that beautiful song that Frank Ocean briefly covers on Channel Orange. It's nice!
Sad song from Noname. She sounds like she's gonna cry.
What I love about this song most is the part when the snare drum comes in on an off-beat at the very end of the song.
I literally heard this song on an advert and looked it up, and ended up listening to the album a bunch when I was doing a workaway in Germany. It's a pretty standard feel-good song but I like it.
Gorgeous music for the camp area. I like the choice of instruments and the very warm vibe.
This is a really cool kind of jazz rock which I enjoy. It's pretty intense. The video is great.
Stunning song, I think a cover, which cycles through fifths like classical music. I love how melodramatic it is. 'Are the sounds of distant drumming just the fingers of your hand?'
Music from Dudley's stage in this edition of Street Fighter. Dudley is a british butler and boxer and everyone agrees is very cool. His theme is pure, classic house!
A great, cute little instrumental track from a later Beach Boys album. I'm a fan.

dailysoundtracks.album: The Legend of Zelda: Link's Awakening
dailysoundtracks.description: One of the most iconic scenes in the original game has these two on the beach speculating about if there is a world beyond the sea. The remake handled it perfectly by having the sparse instruments just like the original, and then coming in with lovely warm strings for a second round. I just love it.

dailysoundtracks.track: I Turned Your Picture To The Wall
dailysoundtracks.description: A bonus track from this album which for many years didn't have a name on my mp3 player. I always liked it.
One of my all-time favourite karaoke tracks.
I spent many years enjoying and dancing to this song before I realised it's about depression!

dailysoundtracks.track: The Last Of The Melting Snow
dailysoundtracks.description: A favourite of 15 year old Fraser's. I still come back to this band.
What I like about this song is they slow down during the chorus and it sounds like it feels when you are that miserable. It's one of those songs that you can lean into your sadness with.
The absolute vibes from the first area of this incredible game are just so good. Love everything about it.
A great song I listened to a lot in 2025. Something about that melody and chord sequence really resonates. The band is US American even but I associate it with my trips to Mexico.
Many years ago my friend Elly recommended me this album from obscure Scottish indie artist King Creosote. This album track is what stuck with me the most.

The most incredible remix from this whole soundtrack. I freaked out when I heard they took my favourite Rainbow Road music and turned it into a disco track! I got so excited that I made a remix of it and played it in a DJ set at KitKat (one of my greatest achievements in life).
Obsessed with this track from Amy where she complains about her mate's boyfriend coming over and smoking all her weed. She just doesn't give a fuck.
Listened to this song on repeat when I was a teenager. I don't know anything about this indie band but somehow they ended up on my ipod.

This for me was when I realised that music can embody super particular vibes. For me this one really embodied the image of pilots and air hostesses walking with purpose through the airport and flying to their destinations. It's classy. They have places to be. I even remember making a playlist of exactly this vibe, consisting largely of these scandinavian electro-pop bands.
I don't claim to be a huge Fiona fan but this song I really enjoy.
This is my favourite Robyn song. Love that is has two halves. Also love the video.
A banger song about reflecting on a relationship where she just allowed all this annoying shit to happen and kicking herself for it, but still not finding it in her to actually hate them.
Love this huge vibe of a track. Maybe you can put this on when you're driving in a car and the conversation and run dry.
Now this is a deep cut. It's just a great chill, vibe. Almost definitely used for a love scene in an old film. Maybe you can put it on when seducing your lover.
I really enjoy this indie band from ages ago. This album I associate with narrowboats.
Representing Finland over here, we have some singing in French and a super vibes-heavy track. Something about a man with a deep voice speaking in French just does something to me.

This song is inextricably linked with SSX 3, where this is one of the songs on the radio station. That's how I learned about Röyksopp! It's the perfect music for skiing and snowboarding. Don't tell anyone because I know it's dangerous but once I put in earphones and listened to this while skiing down a mountain on a family holiday.
This Swedish singer-songwriter did a few Frank Sinatra covers and it's genuinely how I learned about some of his famous songs.
Love this one specifically for the clarinet. The band kinda annoy me, ever since they made that one bad song on Janelle Monáe's otherwise perfect album.
This song is the blueprint for her reinvention as a disco queen. I don't think the What's Your Pleasure revolution would have happened if it weren't for this song. It's so good!
Some experimental electronic music that was popular back then. I always enjoyed this album track.
Shout-outs to Malaysian singer Yuna for collaborating with Little Simz for this track. It's great. And I just love the music video.

Is there is a band who makes better music for spy films than Portishead? Such an incredible, specific vibe. This song is also part of an EP which scores their own little spy film 'To Kill A Dead Man', which I really enjoy.
Devastating track from Anohni. 'I have a glint in my eye... I think I wanna die'. I mean just imagine death by drone. Drones these soulless, humanless killing machines. And gosh the video.
A sad song I used to listen to a lot. Really nice.
Listened to this as a teenager. I don't know who Susan is because I like that piano part.
This is a bop, it's a bit all over the place and the vocals I was unsure about at first. But it's great!

This is a very chaotic band and I'm not on board with a lot of their stuff but I do really like this song, especially the spoken word part in the middle. Turns out Róisín Murphy directed the surreal music video!
I'm in it for the bassline and the section with the piano and strings. Gorgeous.
Gorgeous 70s folky song, reminiscent of Glen Campbell. You can almost feel the wind against your skin when you listen to this.
I was a Breakbot fan before they got a viral hit! This is my favourite track from them. I love to DJ it. Love the vocals.
A fun little duet! Love it. Great singing from both of them.
Great, smooth, vibey and epic. Music to enjoy on a warm evening on your bicycle.
Heavy vibes and great bass. Nice little voice sample which I really enjoy.

This song is such a specific kind of vibe that I love. 90s electronica goodness. Love Madonna for pivoting to this for album n.7! Leo told me that this video is what he thought taking drugs for the first time would be like.
Just a perfect pop song from Róisín. She's great.
Love that bassline. So funky. A huge hit!
Beautiful little track from icon Sophie. Love it when she says 'ahhh is that a teardrop in your eye?' so coyly.
Groovy track about when the club's about to close. It's a bop.
Groovy remake of the Jungle Japes theme for that gorgeous sunset silhouette level. So fun!

dailysoundtracks.album: Mario Kart World (New Super Mario Bros. Wii)
dailysoundtracks.description: Insane glow-up to THE iconic desert track from 2D Mario. So fun. I love desert music so much.
The spotify algorithm gave me this one some years back and I really like it. Intense.
This is a mega moody and vibey track from a mega moody and vibey album that I constantly find myself coming back to. The video is a bit disconcerting though.
Just a great groove from the DS era. Takes me back.

At the end of the original Pilotwings there is a sudden tone shift as you have to rescue some allies who have been captured at war. It's the final mission of the game and you fly a helicopter. It's suddenly very intense! For this one track, Koji Kondo, the legend himself, actually did the song. He tended to supervise the newer composers and do just one track on each soundtrack!

Cool, psychedelic track from an excellent album that I loved when I was a teenager. I associate this music with playing World of Warcraft! I was put onto it by friends I made on the internet back then. The climax in the middle of the song is one for the ages. It sounds like how it feels after you cum.
Beautiful, sad song from this great Scottish band. The singer tragically died early, and now this song has taken on a new meaning.
Peaceful and vibey track with nice vocals. Music to enjoy the sunset to.
Gorgeous little peaceful music with a great chord sequence, there to greet you at the end of each day in Pikmin when you fly into space.

This is such a fun, spooky little cheeky track. It's early Spellling, before she got access to the whole live band, and it's fucking great. It's like music for driving your toy spaceship through space.
I'm in it for that awesome guitar riff in the second half of the song. It's a sexy song.
A fun, funky track. I love his accent when he sings/raps.
This is a fun track. I love the first part when she says 'I'm good, I'm calm'. Very in my lane and moisturised vibes.
Disco magic from the producer from Hot Chip. This song is extra special because of the last two minutes when the vibes hit. It's like when the drugs kick in.
Sometimes it's nice to have a slow burner like this. Great music for cycling.
Absolutely incredible song that I love and adore. So ahead of its time. Magical music. The video is insane and truly a 10/10.
I listened to this song for like 20 years but only now as I write this and watch the video do I realise it's a song about climate change. I love the vibraphone part.

A slower deep cut from Janelle's seminal sci-fi album the ArchAndroid. This song is about many things but I interpret it as that feeling of finding a place of safety where you can love without fear. I love the part when she describes her encounter with the lady with long grey hair. When she performed this song live she would paint a picture of the 'electric lady', which would go on to inspire her next album.
Banger music from the UFO level. It just keeps going. It keeps evolving. So much to enjoy!
This music is so fun. Real alien music, exemplified beautifully by the trippy, alien-filled music video. Obsessed.

Shoutouts to Lily Allen for making a song about a guy who's bad in bed. I love the video, specifically this one point towards the end when the guitarist mouths 'what?' as he realises what the song's about.
This song was huge back then and for good reason, it's a banger. That bassline!
This whole album is full of great tracks but I especially love this chill one. I love the gentle harpsichord and piano combo into the section with the strings. Gorgeous.
A very rousing song from Brittany! Love the keyboards and synths.
Making a chorus out of 'this world is drunk and the people are mad' is a vibe. It's like a song about hopeless frustration.
I learned about this song when it played during the ending of a visual novel called Harold Halibut. It may be German-language but it's a great, chill vibe with mysterious lyrics.

I don't know much about this band but I think the algorithm gave me this in recommendation some years ago. It's such a chill vibe, and I later learned that Kero Kero Bonito have them down as an inspiration!
Just a nice track I've always enjoyed. Karen O is from the Yeah Yeah Yeah's but I originally knew her from the 'Where the wild things are' soundtrack and I've been a fan since.
This is a bass guitar song made with harmonics. It's a bit of a ride. Definitely not for everyone. Very ethereal.
This is, as far as I'm concerned THE bass guitar song. It's funky as hell.
It's a catchy song about Paris, Texas!
This Gorillaz album is great. This is one of my favourites from it. It's all about the big washout in the second half of the song.
I heard that this song was meant to be originally 'me and my girl' but it was misheard. I like it, and the video is great.
I really like the chill chorus of this song. Trippy video too.
Gorgeous Brazilian soul. Wow.
This little EP is amazing, her voice is super nice, and I love the classic soul vibe. What I love the most about this song is the part at the end when she's really wailing.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Yasuhisa Baba, Chika Sekigawa, Naoko Mitome
dailysoundtracks.description: Great final level music from one of my favourite childhood games.
No idea what this song is about but it's just so lovely.
Great track from a great album. I love how sinister this sounds. I especially like that there's a bit of singing at the beginning, but then it's just this intense instrumental.
A very positive, summery song that I've enjoyed for many summers.
Huge vibe. I remember hearing this for the first time and realising that it instantly became the best shop music from a video game ever. I feel like it's inspired by Sonic CD Tidal Tempest.
Came for the chord sequence, stayed for the voice and the lyrics. She's amazing.
Love this super intense track from the final (super difficult) level of the only bomberman game I really got into.
I don't know too much about this band but I really enjoy the bridge of this song with the voiceover. Video is fun.
A super fun track from my childhood. Banger.
The first level from this legendary game. The soundtrack is house-inspired and a huge bop front to back.

dailysoundtracks.album: Donkey Kong Country 2: Diddy's Kong Quest
dailysoundtracks.description: This is hands down the best lava level music out there.
Beautiful song from Laura. She's singing about women!

dailysoundtracks.artist: Go Ichinose, Junichi Masuda, Hitomi Sato, Morikazu Aoki
dailysoundtracks.description: What an intro! I loved this as a child so much. So jazzy and unlike any other snow/ice theme.
Huge bop from the N64. So much to enjoy about this positive party vibe. Brings back great memories of this game.

This song really reminds me of playing World of Warcraft at the age of 16. The first couple of Coldplay albums are really great and I've learned that this song is one of the very first songs they ever made.
I used to be a fan of this in primary school! I think that's when I must have had my phase of 'adult contemporary' music. Listening to it again now, this chorus is great.
A very peaceful song. A little bit haunting. There is indeed a small build up to Feist's amazing ending to the song.

dailysoundtracks.track: The Loneliness of a Tower Crane Driver
dailysoundtracks.description: I'm just gonna say it I actually enjoy a lot of Elbow songs. I don't think tower crane drivers are particularly lonely and this song feels over the top but I can't help but be moved by the key change at the end.
Beautiful track that I have listened to countless times over the years. No idea what it's about.
A sad song. He got on Rhye because he knew he's writing a sad song. A real match made in heaven.
Such a great, sexy groove. Love the line about watching porn in the hotel room.
Love it when he says 'let him have it!'. Love the harmonic shift in the bridge.
The best thing about this song is the minute and a half long intro. Such a build up. Love it.

Banger track, so fun and so gay and the video especially is amazing. It actually captures the Berlin party vibe! There are so many great shots in it. Watching it makes me want to take drugs and party right now.
Huge house track. Song of the summer.
Catchy song that I really enjoy, even if it's a bit cringe maybe.
Ultra intense track from this band evoking a strange combination of vibes, 'emergency at sea' and 'beach holiday'

dailysoundtracks.album: Super Smash Bros. Ultimate (Metal Gear 2: Solid Snake)
dailysoundtracks.description: Such a great, intense af track, with this super nice guitar riff at the climax of the track.
So moody, so sexy, so gorgeous. This is the song that got me into them because I was entranced by this classy, sensual vibe.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Kenta Nagata, Hajime Wakai, Toru Minegishi, Koji Kondo
dailysoundtracks.album: The Legend of Zelda: The Wind Waker
dailysoundtracks.description: This track is so catchy and nostalgic. I think the original Kakariko theme is great but this takes it to new heights.
Absolutely obsessed and loving the Kylie vibes on this one. The song just sounds so cheeky and fun. Love it.
Huge track. Love it. Have DJ'd it multiple times! I even saw them play it live in a bar in Prenzlauer Berg, and even Kuntessa herself was there.
One of my all-time favourite K.K. tracks, and I think an underrated one. This one is a bolero, so romantic and gorgeous. Can you imagine me getting a boyfriend where I could unironically put this on?
This album was huge when it came out. I also really enjoyed it, and this is the best song from it. I'm kinda blown away though that this video has 100 million views.
An ethereal song about being on ecstasy. I listened to it so many times when I was a teenager and I straight up didn't know what ecstasy was back then.
Unlocked a core memory hearing this. Turns out this is a DDR remix of the first level more Gradius 2! That's why there's that chiptune part. Man I loved this so much when I was like 16.
I'm in it for the hypnotic drums and bassline. It's classic house goodness.
Melancholic, serious music. Music that starts playing when you tell someone 'I need to speak to you'.

This song is all over the place. I learned about it from a funny video of a dildo going past a bunch of people on a airport luggage belt while she sings 'nobody nobody nobody' over again. Makes me chuckle everytime. The song is great.

dailysoundtracks.album: Desire, I Want To Turn Into You
dailysoundtracks.description: This is such a lovely, warm song. I like the bagpipes.

dailysoundtracks.album: Ancient Dreams in a Modern Land
dailysoundtracks.description: The lyrics are a bit on the nose but honestly I get it. She's frustrated and so am I.
Love this groovy, intense song. Love the Kill Bill-esque alarm sounds.
Love this party remix of the normal Phendrana Drifts theme. At first I didn't like it but now I'm hooked. Love that atmospheric medley over the ever repeating bassline.
Classic one hit wonder from the UK. Such a shame she never made it outside of this song, it's so good.
When I watch this video I get a very strong impression of this awkward british guy living in America. I'm kinda hooked on the video even though I don't think it's good. The song is great though.
The lyric 'I've got glitter on my clit' says it all. She's an icon.
I love how positive-sounding this is. And then some great lyrics too. Always loved this.
This and the (ablaze) version are both class. So good. Love the vibes of the fast paced bongos and dreamlike wind instruments.
Beautiful piano ballad from Norah.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Manaka Kataoka, Yasuaki Iwata, Hajime Wakai
dailysoundtracks.album: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
dailysoundtracks.description: This is the music from the extra dungeon that came with the DLC. It takes the normal shrine theme and turns it into some genuinely nice.
Shoutouts to Dorian Electra for writing multiple songs about romancing your bro. I'm obsessed with how they sing 'one on one' like you know exactly what they want!
The doomer anthem of our times. This was the first time I saw AI videos for real at scale. Appropriate.
Great track that I've always enjoyed. Especially enjoy the bridge towards the end.
UFO 50 is bursting at the seams with excellent music! I appreciate how they unafraid to throw in microtonal shit in there. This is maybe the weirdest game, Mooncat, with some lovely fairy-like music.
I loved this song since forever because it's on one of the most entertaining and amazing albums ever. Just now I watched the video for the first time and it's a wild ride.
I used to listen to this album a lot when I was like 15. This one is worth it for list of places alone.
Devastating, desperately sad music. The chord sequence alone is enough to set me off. The location in the game is a place of hopelessness and that is very well reflected in this music.
Just a lovely, cute vibe of a track. Shoutouts to France.

dailysoundtracks.track: The Other Side Of The Game
dailysoundtracks.description: Beautiful story from Erykah about being pregnant with her first child.
Very intense and beautiful music.
Cute little track about going to the cinema, something that I love to do. Love the choice of instruments and synths and samples that this band uses.

I don't know too much about this band but I love that they got these vocalists from Benin on the track! It's 8 young girls from Natitingou and they were asked to come up with vocals for the track. They went for all singing the same notes and singing in French about the importance of work! It's 'le travail' not 'Look away'!
Nothing quite beats this chorus when she belts out 'I just lay down and cry'
I love this frosty sounding song. Something about that percussion makes it feel like alien music. The lyrics are all about how she's totally into an effeminate man.
One of the original songs I heard from one of my favourite singers. Favourite line: 'I picked up a seashell to illustrate my homelessness, but a crab called out of it making it useless'
Nothing quite beats the quiet, confident and regular 'Yes!' that is heard throughout this song. So affirming. And the video is everything.

dailysoundtracks.album: Paper Mario: The Thousand Year Door
dailysoundtracks.description: Beautiful music that plays when you're on the train in Paper Mario at sunset. It only plays like once I think, but it's a big vibe.

dailysoundtracks.album: If My Wife New I'd Be Dead
dailysoundtracks.track: I Wanna Be A Cowboy, Baby!
dailysoundtracks.description: A song inspired by frustration of being a woman, and I guess also that vine. It's so catchy and real and I love CMAT so much. I got a T-shirt with this on it and I love it.
Lovely, warm track. Feels like a breeze.

dailysoundtracks.album: Me Chama De Gato Que Eu Sou Sua
dailysoundtracks.description: Gorgeous track from this artist that I love. I watched her play in Berlin and it was fucking great. Also love the vibes of the video.
Huge vibe that I learned about because Andy Armer used to make classic video game music.

dailysoundtracks.album: My Back Was A Bridge For You To Cross
dailysoundtracks.description: This most devastating song. It's about the environment. How is it we have so few songs about the collapse of our environment? This song is just so gorgeous, with the breezy guitar and strings. I've listened to it countless times.
Lovely song from a lovely album. For me this song is very nostalgic.
Beautiful track consisting of a hypnotic string loop with Solange singing over it explaining how she tries to cope.
Super cool hip house track. It's classy and groovy!
Can't remember how I learned about this song but I love it. It's so gorgeous. That chord progression! The singer is British but she moved to Spain and became a pop star there in the 70s. Cool!
I do like Father John Misty and yes this song is a bit on the nose and maybe not very coherent. But like, it's not wrong. A blistering criticism on organised religion.
Beautiful track about being a rockstar's girlfriend. She's singing saying 'please bring me along' but she's the real rockstar. When I was a 11 year old gay boy I thought this was everything.
Shoutouts to Kimbra, man. She has so many great songs. This is a deep cut from a deluxe edition of her first album and it's so gorgeous and otherworldly.

Shoutouts to Carlos Eiene who is this youtuber who for a long time made excellent jazz fusion remixes of all the best vgm tracks. For me this is his Magnum Opus. It sounds a bit off because he uses percussion from in game but that just adds to the charm. The best part is the saxophone reveal towards the end. Absolutely inspired.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Yoshie Arawaka, Yoshi Takayanagi, Nobuyoshi Sano
dailysoundtracks.description: The early Tekken games have amazing soundtracks. This is a highlight, the theme of my fave Tekken character Nina.
Beautiful and tragic
This song is very warm. No idea what it's about but I love it.
Super cute song that reminds me of springtime.
Just a super cute song about twin sisters. I like it when they explain how to pronounce their name in the song.
This band really make me laugh, two matching twinks doing their best to sound like the Beatles. This song did charm me though, especially with the vibraphone sound in the chorus.
I know GEFÄHRLICH to recommend a song so closely related to Schlager but this German Disco track from 1979 is actually great. One of the few German-language songs I tolerate.
This is a friend of mine! She played this at Monarch Bar and it was great.
This is my go-to home workout album and this is the first track. I love that it's this simple guitar riff but by the end they've harmonised it with this epic chord sequence.
This is my favourite from this (excellent) album. Love the bright synth chords in the chorus.
I never played this game but for some reason I made a mix of its soundtrack, largely because I really enjoyed this one particular track. It's just so epic.
Nina Simone said that she finally found freedom when she moved to Liberia. Anaiis thought about freedom meant to Nina and what it means to her and that's how we got this beautiful song.

This was a groundbreaking moment in 1924 when Gershwin made orchestra music inspired by jazz. It's great. I associate it with my childhood! I especially love the final part because for whatever reason I had just the last 5 minutes of this on my mp3 player.

dailysoundtracks.track: Scheherazade: The Young Prince and the Princess
dailysoundtracks.description: This is my favourite symphony! And my favourite part of my favourite symphony is part 3: the prince and princess. I original heard this when playing Civilisation 4 and loved it. Then my friend at the time Tim played it with his orchestra! It's stuck with me ever since. It's so gorgeous!
Learned about this banger disco version of the Nina classic from the final scene in Kill Bill! So epic. Also highly recommend the 16 minute extended version.

This remix sounds nothing like the original track but that's totally fine. I love KKB house, especially when it has piano chords like this. It's got a really cute verse about looking at photographs as well that I really enjoy.
I'm not normally listening to rock music but this one is so great. And I love the lyrics 'I love to come home because I love my wife!'
This song is such a vibe, oh my lord. I feel really in my element when I sing this at karaoke. I also really enjoy the video how the second guy just sits nearby and vibes.
Music that to me sounds like deer running through woodland. From a cool Swedish band that do this kind of atmospheric music.
Beautiful song about navigating your feelings, relationships and sexuality as a young woman. Really gorgeous. Side note, I love that these two always wear glorious yellow.
I think was algorithmically recommended to me during my Spotify years and something about it really resonated with me.
I had a phase of going crazy for this album. Loved this song especially. It's a slow burn.
I always enjoyed this super intense track. Love the beat.
Love this song. I mean this in a nice way but its giving swamp creature vibes. There's this one part towards the end where it modulates and it's just sooooo gorgeous.
Heavenly music. Cool that he got motherfucking Beyoncé to do the backing vocals.
An intense song from local folktronica band Tunng. I associate this with living in Switzerland and discovering the Fire Emblem games.
This band made a bossa nova/brazilian lounge album and I fucking love it. This song is in French, it's a bit cheesy, and I love it.

Shoutouts to Regina for not singing songs about sex and relationships. She sings about literally anything else. This song is about more than just having a bad day, but that hasn't stopped me playing it and singing at the piano on days when I'm feeling emotional.
God, the part two of this song... wow. Amazing chords and strings. Music for when you're struggling.
Always loved this track and this album.
Beautiful song about sadness and frustration. Especially love the strings at the end.
Love this track. Reminds me of the time when I used to play World of Warcraft. Love the disco chorus.
This is the title screen from the game. It starts with 1 minute of beach ambience and then goes into a lovely peaceful track. Love it, have always loved it. This game was my shit.
This track from Jessie........ I rolled my eyes the first time but I keep coming back to it. Steamy diva pop version of the good, the bad and the ugly.... it's a yes from me.
Some sick UK garage music. Love the music video

I'm obsessed with this song and video. Super problematic lyrics in verses and then the chorus is just asking for consent. Great. And the video is very inspiring. This very website is inspired by this band - specifically their songs like this before they started dropping real albums.
I really love this. Such a great hook and I love the ending. Have to say I just watched the music video and it really is weird and bad.
Such a great little pop track! I love the backing vocals. I love the lyrics too.

'I don't wanna go to work today I want to stay home and play video games' - this is a mantra I've carried with me for many years now. This song often comes into my head on a monday morning. Jill Scott understands. This was actually my introduction to her!

I'm obsessed with this chord sequence. And I love Esperanza's verse. I love the little monologue at the end. I once made a piece of artwork with those lyrics on it and my friend Lydia said, 'you do like to be extra about everything don't you', and she's right.
I've listened to this countless times. There's something about it. It sounds so classy, and I love it when she sings 'just rid yourself of him and come get it'
I love a chill song with a drum machine like this. This one is hypnotic with the singing in harmony. My favourite part is the little synth lead that comes in between the singing.
Beautiful and gorgeous music from the SNES. I could listen to this forever. I love that it doesn't really have a key and I especially love the last part with the piano chords.
As an Esperanza Spalding enjoyer I of course love this track with her singing this gorgeous melody of those windy chords.
This song touched me. In it, he reveals that his wings are made of plastic.
I love love love this song and I know all the words. I'm also obsessed with the beautiful twink in the video, the way he looks into my soul.

I learned about this from Sade who covered this. But the original, wow. It's just a guy with a drum machine & a little organ in 1972 and it's this stark and soulful. Also, it's the sample from Drake's smash hit Hotline Bling!
Found out about this song during my phase of listening to French electropop. Love the video.
Something about 7pm, it's so melancholic. Definitely the best hourly theme from this game.
This is my go-to salsa song! There are many that I like but I often come back to this.
This song, just like the whole album, is so breezy and light and lush and soulful. It's about the state of the world when it was released in the 70s. The whole album is wildly relevant today.

He recorded this after he was arrested for cruising in public toilets. It's the best comeback after a scandal ever, he really just said fuck you, fuck police, I just wanna fuck guys. The video is great.
A dancefloor classic. I first knew about it thanks to Horsemeat Disco. Now I DJ it all the time because it's the perfect disco song.
A total classic. Everyone loves it. The kinda music to get everyone singing on the dancefloor.
This song is so unlike normal pop songs, especially in terms of key changes. I like it though. Just constant key changes! It's secretly a Bee Gees song.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Honey Dijon (feat. Dave Giles II, Cor.Ece & Mike Dunn)
dailysoundtracks.description: Amazing track for the dancefloor. Everyone loves it.
The queen of disco is here. I love her and I love this album. This song is my favourite, I love the strings.
Sad song. Really sad song about someone passing.

The song is good but we have to talk about the absolute insanity which is the ending credits to Bayonetta when this plays, and she does a whole 3-minute ridiculous choreography to it. Slay queening constantly for no particular audience apart from the camera. It is totally unhinged, inexplicable how it came to be, and I've watched it like 20 times.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Yoshito Sekigawa, Shoh Murakami, Yoshiaki Kimura, Hiroki Morishita, Fumihiro Isobe
dailysoundtracks.description: Absolute banger of a battle theme. Pumps me up so much. This game has many variations of this song (all great) but this is the best one.
This song will always make me think of Victor. It reminds me of another song that I can't put my finger on. Very melancholic. To be enjoyed on special occasions only.
Beautiful and tragic song. Music video is even more tragic.
Such a moody song! Love her voice and love the lyrics.

dailysoundtracks.album: Ecco the Dolphin: Defender of the Future
dailysoundtracks.description: Ecco the Dolphin has excellent soundtracks. It's honestly incredible that these games exist. You are just a dolphin swimming round, it's insanely hard and there is no information. And there are aliens and stuff. And Tim Follin is out here making the best music ever for it. Sit back and enjoy these 10 minutes, they will fly by.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Hajime Wakai, Mahito Yokota, Shiho Fujii, Takeshi Hama
dailysoundtracks.album: The Legend of Zelda: Skyward Sword
dailysoundtracks.description: The most beautiful desert music out there, and I'm a real fan of desert music. It has no right to be this hard-hitting. This version contains both present and past versions and it's one of my most listened to tracks from the Nintendo Music app.

dailysoundtracks.album: The Legend of Zelda: Majora's Mask
dailysoundtracks.description: Beautiful excerpt of the ending music, when you can hear this song that you practice with the band in full. It's great! It's a smooth jam, but it's also very melancholic.

Great track from a band I used to listen to. This is so intense and the climax at the end is just so heavenly. Looking at their youtube presence it doesn't look like they made it big - I'm not sure how I found them

This band is really great, they do excellent jazz fusion stuff, and the original of this track is also amazing. But I don't know what this remixer was smoking but this track is so mind-bendingly insane and good.
Such a gorgeous song, perfect for sunsets on the beach with silhouettes of palm trees. I also really enjoy the ancient video of her performing the song.
This song sits firmly on my babymaking playlist. If the whispered 'come on' doesn't do it for you then I don't know what to tell you. This is the best sounding porn music ever.
It's a classic from Fleetwood Mac but I'm unafraid to admit that I only know this song from the M&S adverts from when I was a teenager.
It's the desert music from Pokemon! It's so fun.

This music is from an album in which she puts Lewis Carroll's long poem 'The Hunting of the Snark, an Agony in Eight Fits' into music. She takes the best bits and turns them literally into lyrics over this string-heavy soul music. The whole album is great.

One of my first Bonobo tracks back when I was becoming a Bonobo fan. What a vibe this song is! So intense and chill at the same time. I love love love the strings at the end of the song. And Bajka is great.
Early Bon Iver really takes me back. This whole album is such a particular winter vibe that I just love and often come back to.
Gorgeous rendition of this song featuring an amazing circle of fifths chord progression. The build up? Insane. I love the drama of this song.
Heartbreaking song. It's about thinking you saw the person you were thinking about in the park, but of course not, because they wouldn't know where in London to find you.
This band is great, it's some brits making hypnotic electronic music with silly lyrics. This one has a funny story to it.

I feel almost nervous about featuring this band on my website because so much of there music is positively creepy. But I've listened to their albums so many times and this song too. It's for when I'm in a particular kind of mood. If you like the song, check out the rest of the album.

I lift weights in the gym to Big Top Bop. It's simply doesn't get more hardcore than Big Top motherfucking Bop and yes it's the boss theme of a cartoon seal. No other boss music in the game comes even close to this, the boss of world 1. Big Top Bop forever.
One of my first ever Hot Chip tracks before I became a fan. Love the warmth. Love the sunset ending
Great track from a great album. This one takes me to a very specific place
Fun song from this britpop band
Childhood British band. Their album is very slow and sad but I listened to it a lot. This first track is super ominous
It's a classic. It's catchy and great for karaoke
Gorgeous, sensuous, stunning music. Babymaking music.
Wow, possibly one of the sexiest songs out there. And the video is WORTH YOUR TIME! The way this song ends is wild to me. The way he looks in the camera right at the end. What's really going on?

dailysoundtracks.artist: Hirokazu Ando, Jun Ishikawa, Yuuta Ogasawara, Yuki Shimooka
dailysoundtracks.description: I love modern kirby soundtracks for their frenetic and harmonic vibes. They often have very juicy chord progressions and you love them like me, go check out the B section of this track!
Huge vibe! Hypnotic, almost.

The lyrics in this song consist of two repeated lines: 'all the feelings you've got for me, it's like for a dog'. She just repeats it over and over. It makes me think about how dogs can't really communicate, like she's actually putting herself in the shoes of a dog. And she sounds so upset!
This artist is known for cool, very audible lyrics in his songs, but this song is just repeating the same thing over and over again. It's got a great build up and release.

dailysoundtracks.album: Desire, I Want To Turn Into You
dailysoundtracks.description: Her most ethereal and gorgeous song. She does all kinds of arty music but this I love the most.
Beautiful, meditative song. I especially love the second part half way through with the thick chords
For me a standout track on this soundtrack. The piano sounds positively icy.
I've been a long time fan of this local punk singer from Southampton. This is his best song, about his mate who died.
Childhood song! I misheard many of these lyrics back then. This song contributes heavily to the sunset beach palm tree vibe that I love in my fashion

Dionne collaborated with Burt Bacharach many times in her career. His songs are complex and interesting and she's also great. This song is so melancholic and I've been a fan ever since listening to the Stranglers cover of it.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Kohta Takahashi, Hiroshi Okubo, Asuka Sakai
dailysoundtracks.description: Banging classic house track from this PS1 game. This era of playstation had some incredible dance music. When I'm feeling strong I will DJ this track
Great house-inspired credits music from this obscure DS game. Can't remember how this crept onto my playlist, I definitely haven't played it. Worth your time though
What a catchy track. I've put this on party playlists ever since it came out
A perfect pop song from one of my favourite divas. I love this rhythmic synth line, I don't know how to describe it but it's kinda hypnotic.

An insane introduction to this parody of a stereotypical victory song. It's so Mario Kart Wii coded that it hurts. I'm obsessed with it. I used it for multiple trailers that I made for smash tournaments.
Standout track from this game, what a magical and mysterious vibe!

Moody song from this band that doesn't normally make this kind of music. The video is captivating, even if I don't find the guy hot. I actually think the girl is insanely sexy in this video. The shot circling her with her wet hair near the beginning... incredible
One of my favourite bands came out with some new singles and they are so moody and great. This one is an obsession of mine just because of the bassline alone.
Great track about a lady finally having good sex with a man. The video is awesome.

The Japanese/EU version of this soundtrack might be obscure but it actually inspired a million other soundtracks. Music like this can be heard in Deltarune for example. It's a specific kind of Japanese fusion music, kinda hard to describe.

Music from an incredible artist that I only listen to when I'm feeling devastated. It's devastating music. The whole album is exclusively for particularly bad events. This particular song is about a feeling of acute loneliness.
Lovely song about feeling sad and lonely, and listening to a song by Gillian Welch and feeling better. I also feel way better after listening to music. Especially heartwarming music like this

I had a real Tee Lopes moment when I realised that this guy who uploaded remixes of old Sonic songs made the incredible official Sonic Mania soundtrack! His remixes from Sonic 1 and 2 are so good, and this is one of my favourites.
This song has such an amazing build up. Great drums. Love the guitar riffs. Makes you want to shake your shoulders

dailysoundtracks.track: You got time and I got money
dailysoundtracks.description: It's the most romantic song of the year. It's such a vibe. She doesn't even sing in key and it's totally fine. She probably recorded this at 4am

dailysoundtracks.artist: Asuka Hayazaki, Atsuko Asahi, Hajime Wakai
dailysoundtracks.description: Beautiful track from in my opinion the most beautiful Pikmin game. Love the bassoon
It's a 70s rock band except they made harpsichord music. The 'golden brown' refers to heroin

dailysoundtracks.album: Animal Crossing: Let's Go To The City
dailysoundtracks.description: Nothing transports me back to childhood more than these hourly themes from the early Animal Crossing games. God damn.
Shoutouts to Erlend Oye, who after being trapped in Italy for the pandemic, started a band. This is a cover of an old electronic song that he was once featured on
I used to love Simon & Garfunkel when I was a teenager and listening to songs like this takes me back to that time. I associate it with the books I was reading at that time

I found this video which combines two tracks from this soundtrack which plays during the part of the game where you are visiting by the ghost of your mother in a field of sunflowers. A poster of this moment hangs in my living room
A Swedish artist that I've followed for a long time and saw live once. This song, to me, is about giving up
This album immediately makes me calm down, and although this song might be the simplest of all the songs, somehow it's the one I come back to most
This song is an encapsulation of Jens's time in Australia and I love it. The song feels like watching a film about his life
It's a beautiful, positive, peaceful song about expected a baby!
Love this soundtrack so much. This is a map theme from Nohr near the beginning. It's brooding and forboding
Hello, what is this insane music playing in this very below average game? I mean what a vibe. It's giving Streets of Rage
Obscure soundtrack but it's got some great tracks. This volcano music from a game I literally haven't played is something I regularly come back to. It has so many phases and takes you so many places
What insane music for an arcade game. Gets me so hyped
Nice song that for some reason gave me Wind Waker earth temple vibes

dailysoundtracks.album: I Put A Spell On You
dailysoundtracks.track: I Put A Spell On You
dailysoundtracks.description: Huge track for karaoke. So intense and amazing. The saxophone
This track just has such a great hook. I often find myself humming it
This song immediately transports me back to this beautiful, melancholy game. This music is so gorgeous. No percussion, just vibes
This is a rhythm game full of bangers, but it also had a very serious story, and this track that appears towards the end of the game is when shit gets real. I fucking love it
The ultimate chillout track. Nothing is more chill. It's also kinda dramatic at the same time
This is such a fun, feel good song. I listen to this when I'm on my own and in a good mood
This soundtrack has a unique ambient electronic vibe, but it's also very emotional. This music plays during the 'bad ending'
What a creepy and special vibe this old PS1 music is. Composed by the legendary Yoko Shimamura, I'm her biggest fan
Is this music a bit cheesy? Yes. Do I still love it? Yes. It's the part where you infiltrate the psyche of the corrupt prime minister
One of the few German language songs I tolerate. Incredible vibes from these Germans
One of the few German language songs that I tolerate. It's a great vibe
Such a lovely and melancholic song. Great music for when you're solo travelling
My favourite song from one of my favourite Mexican singers. She's amazing
This soundtrack is full of vaporwave-style bangers and you know I was vibing my socks off the whole time I was playing
Just a great track that I've always loved from this band

I am obsessed with this song, but especially the video. The video is insane. The green screen, the shots of louie just nodding, the laggy audience, musicians playing something else, the bloodshot red eyes, there isn't even a saxophone in the song! The video is iconic and I'm obsessed.

I'm obsessed with the vibe of this track. It plays in a futuristic utopian city. It's a city you come to right at the end of the game after lots of hardships and arriving just feels so good. And wow, the insane harmony in this song sets me off
Such a great synth pop soundtrack with a special kind of vibe. In the game you're serving drinks in a cyberpunk bar and you get to select the music
I left the menu music playing on this game so often because I was vibing so hard with it. I learned this on the piano and all

This song is so weird and wonderful. What I love about it most is the relatively simple chord sequence, then followed by this reharmonisation with the guitar in the final minute of the song. The vocals from Sleaford Mods is just wild as well. I can only imagine what the vibes in the room were while recording
Gorgeous, peaceful track. This song will clear your skin and purify your bad feelings
This track is a great snapshot of a moment in 2020 when the black lives matter protests were happening. I love it
This is by far the insane and epic boss battle music you'll hear this month. Prepare to get your socks knocked off

dailysoundtracks.album: Super Smash Bros. Brawl (Fire Emblem: Gaiden)
dailysoundtracks.description: Obscure song from a Japanese-only game which became an iconic moment in the Subspace Emissary when you go through the desert with Marth. It still slaps all these years later
This is a special track. It's got heavy Trainspotting vibes. Love this combination of dance music and spoken word
Hard-hitting track. Love how it takes a minute for the vocals to come in. I also just really like rapping over sad song beats
A sad, slow and beautiful song. It's about loneliness
I mean it's a classic. You'll know it instantly. Funnily enough, Ca7riel & Paco Amoroso played this at their concert
This soundtrack is very harsh, right up until the very end when you get a beautiful piece of piano music
Just love the voice that says 'Chris' at the start of this song. And it's a bop

This indie game had no business going so hard on the soundtrack. I love all the music from the opening area 'Mafia Town' because it sounds like Windfall Island and also has these beautiful chord sequences. This is the scootin' variant which plays when you ride your scooter.

This fan game never happened because of a cease and desist from Nintendo, but that didn't stop the composer releasing an entire soundtrack from it and it fucking slaps. This track is so intense and captivating, and I can only imagine what kind of battle must have been planned for it.
This band became a weird normie pop thing but their early music was pretty wacky and I was into it

dailysoundtracks.track: High Above Tassel Town (The Sky Bridge)
dailysoundtracks.description: Incredible desert music from Shantae. I especially love that opening section
Gorgeous music. I love the 'sayonara' at the end
Gorgeous track from one of my favourite artists. The part with the whistling at the end is everything

dailysoundtracks.track: You can Hear the Cry of the Planet
dailysoundtracks.description: Wow, what a moment in this Final Fantasy when you get to the Forgotten City and you hear this mysterious synth sound. So ominous
The Streets of Rage 4 soundtrack is fucking sublime. This is just the opener! And it's this epic!
I had a big phase of absolutely loving this band back in university. Turns out they got huge in asia since then, good for them

dailysoundtracks.album: Outer Wilds: Echoes of the Eye
dailysoundtracks.description: This plays at the very end of one of my all time favourite games. I've listened to it countless times. If you know, you know
I had my Tom Misch phase. I really like his voice. I listened to this song a lot in 2019
When I first heard this song I thought it was a woman from London singing but no it's an American dude. I quite like the band but this song in particular means a lot to me
Such an intense song and one of the best music videos ever
The chillest and grooviest music you could ever imagine for going to court. Makes me wanna go to court. If I was a lawyer I would play this at parties
Arguably some of the best desert music ever. My jaw dropped when I heard it in game.

This music plays at the end of the game and it's at that point I realised the soundtrack was done by this old band I used to like called 'British Sea Power' (now renamed to just 'Sea Power'). The little piano part...
This song from Noname has grown on me the most of all her songs. When I heard her play it live and heard how intense it really is, I was honestly a bit blown away.

dailysoundtracks.album: We got it from Here... Thank You 4 Your Service
dailysoundtracks.description: I really love this super fun and chill hip-hop album and this might be favourite track on it. Super cool how different rappers drift in and out
I like rap music especially when the production is warm, beautiful, soulful, melancholic like this
Huge track from this lesser known Street Fighter game. Saxophones are going hard and I can't wait to play this in a DJ set

Obsessed with this song of two dudes enjoying their holidays on the beach, talking nonsense about girls. The video is great, how he doesn't even play the guitar. The little banter at the end of the song.
I fantasise about singing this song in my house while pensively thinking about life
Absolutely outrageous silky smooth song that is so delicious that it could be by the band Sade. Incredible that it was on this obscure Nintendo 64 game.
This is a song about a woman and her vibrator. It's great
Is it country music? Maybe. We forgive though because of the strings during the ending

dailysoundtracks.artist: Junichi Masuda, Go Ichinose, Morikazu Aoki
dailysoundtracks.description: A classic. The intro... the build up to the drums... this meant everything to me as a child
I don't know much about this band but this song really does it for me. It feels like driving music

dailysoundtracks.album: The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess
dailysoundtracks.description: Haunting and gorgeous music from the beginning of the game. It's chill but it has an edge to it.
A song about learning that things are fake. There's this great moment when she screams: 'what do we have here?? could this be a forgery?!'
Couldn't tell you what this song is about but it's a bop and when I saw them in concert not too long ago I was so happy when this played this song from a million years ago
This generation has tons of amazing music but for me the standout is this absolute bop when you go to change your outfit in the game
Music arguably more famously used in the minigames of new super mario bros on the DS, this Luigi casino music is fucking great

The song is great but the real star is the music video. It's so random, it's like stock footage and if you know me you know I love stock footage. A very certain vibe that I totally subscribe to. Honestly it's giving AI generated, even though it was before all that.
Awesome sci-fi music that I learned about from a playlist that Magdalena Bay made of their influences.
This band make the silkiest smoothest music ever, normally with the singer's breathy girl voice, but this song has a deep-voiced man all of a sudden and wow. Fuerte
Lots of beautiful music from this soundtrack to enjoy, but Alicia's theme (also the main theme) stuck with me the most

dailysoundtracks.artist: Toru Minegishi, Manaka Kataoka, Shiho Fujii
dailysoundtracks.description: This music doesn't sound like this in game, it's a special mix for the soundtrack and it's a huge banger. I can already picture the crowd cheering me as I step on and off the balance board to this music.

My friend sent me a cool video of 'how to dance in Berghain' and this was the background music. I ended up watching the clip loads of times because I loved how she said 'anything can happen at 19:00!'
Super fun and funky track! It's also a bit weird I guess but I find it super optimistic and lovely
Incredible music to bring a tear to your eye. Contains the lyric 'I need you more than want you, and I want you for all time'. Stunning song

One of my favourite soundtracks ever! This is potentially the ultimate ice level music. It's impossibly groovy and has this insane synth solo. This game truly lives up to the original Sonic games I love it so much.
This band seem so cool, local Brits who I feel like I would get on well with. I love when she refers to herself by name in the song.
Beautiful little track that I keep finding myself coming back to

dailysoundtracks.album: Ice, Death, Planets, Lungs, Mushrooms and Lava
dailysoundtracks.description: A total jam! Nothing exemplifies its vibes more than its video which features this guy just having the best holiday ever.

Be warned this artist has wildly explicit lyrics and involves him playing the part of a pathetic man singing about prostitutes. If you're fine with that, you should totally enjoy this track and the album!!
This is a throwback for those whoever played the original Runescape. it's the music that plays on the tutorial island and it transports me back to 2004.

dailysoundtracks.album: The Legend of Zelda: Link's Awakening
dailysoundtracks.description: This remade gameboy music has no right to be as dramatic and harrowing as this. It's the part of the game where you learn the truth of the island and suddenly when you listen to this you feel the gravity of the situation.

dailysoundtracks.track: Concerning the UFO sighting near Highland, Illinois
dailysoundtracks.description: I use this song to test headphones. It's the start of an amazing album and I love it.
The weakest Pikmin game it may be but its music is just as good as the rest of the series. They threw in some bossa nova music for the abandoned construction site area and you know me
An insanely over the top song about keeping running even though you have a gash in your leg. It's actually great
I have a fond memory of listening to this when I was skiing down a mountain. Huge vibes, especially towards the end of the track.
This is a song about panic and cowardice. I once had an idea to make a bunch of music inspired by different raw emotions like panic, because the music can almost make you feel them again.
Can't remember how I got into this local folky band but they have some amazing music and for a time they were my favourite band. This one is one of their more magical tracks
Beautiful, slow, lilting ballad with piano and horns. It's the guy from Grizzly Bear! This was a recommendation from another artist I love and kick-started a dive into indie music when I was like 14

dailysoundtracks.track: Dry Dry Desert (Mario Kart: Double Dash!!)
dailysoundtracks.description: They turned one of the shittiest mario kart songs into the grooviest track ever. A huge bop!
Devastating music from a genuinely great game.
During my Kimbra phase I got into this artist too and vibed to it loads when I was living in Saarland.
Great city pop vibes from this band! I also really recommend watching all the videos from this album.
Beautiful song that entered my life at a particular time in early 2025. Is Natalie feeling like me?
Beautiful chill out music sent to me by Diego that I come back to often

This game has no right to be as groovy as it is, considering it's a horror-themed game. Castlevania has ended up having the music with the most unique vibe ever. This is just the music for exploring the basement of Dracula's castle.
This song is literally Amy explaining to her boyfriend that having sex with someone else isn't cheating if she was thinking about him the whole time

I had a strong bossa nova phase in 2013 and even bought a cheap guitar to learn to play this. This song also sparked a great conversation and thus a great evening with my Airbnb hosts in Portugal about the pronunciation of the word 'felicidade'.
A swedish band that I used to be in to. This is a track with a great groove that I used to have on repeat.
I had a phase of Swedish pop music in like 2010 and I found lots of cute pop gems from over there. This track is so great and I still come back to it.

A song about how she hates Jamie Oliver but then she defensively tries to explain herself, becoming increasingly manic, asking herself why she has such a visceral reaction, making increasingly wild connections and ultimately screaming 'don't be a bitch the man has kids and they wouldn't like this!'
Obsessed with this song's beautiful chorus. What a chord progression.

This is a remix of 'Fourside' and the credits theme from Earthbound by none other than composer of Shovel Knight himself Jake Kaufman. Back then he was called 'virt' and I was a fan. I should have known that the person who made this insane remix would go on to be a hugely successful video game composer! I'm obsessed with the part where the Title Theme kicks in.

dailysoundtracks.track: Smash Superstars (Super Smash Bros. Melee: Trophy Tussle)
dailysoundtracks.description: There was a time when I was heavily into amateur remixes of video game music on OCRemix. Looking back, a lot of them were shit, it was just so exciting to hear my favourite tunes redone like this. This one is genuinely excellent though!!
Bit of a deep cut from this soundtrack but it's a huge vibe!
Beautiful song with an amazing saxophone part. It's such a vibe. Amazing chord progression. End of the night kind of music.
This is a popular summer hit from 80's Brazil that has made it overseas, but it was for me a gateway drug into the huge world of Brazilian rock and disco! It's so great.
When I was younger I had a Beatles phase, and although I don't really like to listen to them these days, I often come back to this song.
This is such a vast and beautiful song from Brian Wilson. Gorgeous. No idea what the lyrics are about. The last minute of the song is just heavenly.
The most insane video game composer makes a SNES soundtrack on the SNES soundtrack that sounds impossibly good! It's so wild and so good.
Huge fan of St. Vincent in general but this album is my favourite. It's super 70s and I love the vibe and lyrics of this song a lot.

dailysoundtracks.album: Everything That Happens Will Happen Today
dailysoundtracks.description: It's a kind of haunting song and it's really stuck with me for a long time. David Byrne just has something special about him.

dailysoundtracks.album: Pokemon Mystery Dungeon: Blue Rescue Team
dailysoundtracks.description: Wow! This music is so unlike other Pokemon music and just so groovy. Perfect for the final level of this great game.
Epic, symphonic 7 minute banger from Spellling. It's about being 16 and complaining about the boys at school.... and also so much more than that. The whole album is amazing.
Noisy wall of sound from this great band. Big vibes. Love this track.
This is a song for gazing out the window to. A song for re-evaluating your life choices.

dailysoundtracks.album: Animal Crossing: Let's Go To The City
dailysoundtracks.description: An official club remix of the best K.K. Song from the composer and K.K. Slider himself, Totaka
Insane how gorgeous this track is. It's because all three pilotwings games have 10/10 soundtracks and unmatchable vibes.
Who'd have thought that motherfucking Sydney Sprint would end up being my favourite Mario Kart music from this game. The saxophone goes so crazy and I am obsessed.
Super intense track from the original DKC. I've always loved this game's special vibe and it's all thanks to this music.
This song represents that New York ballroom vibe the best from Madonna's Vogue era. It's so great and I love the video too. There's a flamenco breakdown!

One of my all-time favourites. I remember seeing it on top of the pops live on the TV and thinking it was the best thing ever. I had to go have a bath because I was literally 8, but I made Mum promise to tell me if she comes back on the TV again so I could watch it.

dailysoundtracks.track: Molgera (The Legend of Zelda: The Wind Waker)
dailysoundtracks.description: I know the original is great but there's something about the strings in this smash remix which just gets me going. It's intense enough as it is and then it loops and reharmonises the original making it even more intense. I'm obsessed.
Crazy, symphonic SNES soundtrack which goes so much harder than it needs to. The whole first minute is just the intro! Then it loops in this crazy Mozart-ass psychedelic section.
Great disco album from the disco queen. I love the part with 'rosebud'
Hands-down the best music to come out of the NES. I mean, compare this to the fucking super mario theme.
This song is so nostalgic it's crazy! It's genuinely a great track. It was everywhere in the UK in the 00s.

dailysoundtracks.album: Spider-Man and the X-Men in Arcade's Revenge
dailysoundtracks.description: Hello why is this the craziest SNES music to exist ever? Well it turns out the composer is a hidden legend and a technical wizard. Anyway I can't write anything else because I'm currently transcending listening to this music.
What a dark and creepy vibe for a boss fight! Melancholic music for a melancholic game.
Huge disco track that I have DJ'd many times. I am obsessed with the music video though. The outfits, the close ups, the guy (he's hot), the animals, is there a storyline?
This is THE karaoke song. It's a classic. But I especially love the video how she is a total bitch.
An all time classic. I have a memory of someone challenging me to play this on the piano and I didn't know it so I listened and fell in love. Also the Mario Kart World remix is 10/10

Always loved this song because it's so danceable! Looking back now I realise that this was some rare gay representation when I was growing up. I used to think and honestly still do think that the singer looks so hot in the video. Also, great video.
Great chill track from this genuine banger of a soundtrack. The whole OST is great honestly.
Personal little thing, maybe not for everyone but I always loved it. I have listened to this so much.
Banger track that still goes so hard. I think it was a global hit so you can always break this out at Karaoke. They call me Stacy! THAT'S NOT MY NAME!!

dailysoundtracks.album: Super Smash Bros. Brawl (The Legend of Zelda: A Link to the Past)
dailysoundtracks.description: A unique take on a classic from Link to the Past, which has ultimately become iconic thanks to the majesty of the Subspace Emissary
The breakout hit from this band that I loved as a young gay boy. I think this remains their best track.
Love loads of Jamiroquai classic tracks but this is maybe a little less known and is actually a huge banger. There's this part at the end that I love when the strings come in.
A song about discovering your husband has a pussy palace and is a sex addict. I love songs with very concrete themes and this one is just great and catchy too. Shoutouts to Lily Allen she's great.
I'm in it for the chord progression and the minimal Animal Crossing instrumentation. Just trust me it's great.
Great band and great music videos always. I would love to direct and make a music video like this one day.
Sad song. Love the ending. Great album
Great psych rock track from an early Tame Impala album, always find myself coming back to this.
Beautiful music for when you meet your sister in the game. My upstairs neighbour plays this on the piano and I bonded with her about that.
Old song that I loved as a teenager and coming back to it now I realise it's still great. It's got this amazing build up towards the end.
A legendary composer of funky video game music made the soundtrack to a childhood game of mine, Gex! It's so good. This one is from the spaghetti western level.
So many huge tracks from this soundtrack but today I'm serving you their disco banger from the beach level. Would love to DJ this but I feel like it's too much.
Huge hit from a huge (okay very underrated) game.
I heard this song from an advert way back when and actually I love it. Video is great too. The lyrics do not make sense.
Great track from an underrated band featuring a cool spaghetti western break in the middle. Whole album is worth a listen!
The first level from this classic game. It's beautiful.
I love her so much. She really came out with 3 sci-fi concept albums before becoming a megastar. This is her super ambitious debut, it's so good.
This band released an album that's not on streaming! I have the CD. This song is great, it's super funny and features Little Simz!
I was obsessed with this song when it came out and honestly I still am. The video is also great. This band is a heavy inspiration for my website
I love the second verse in this bop. She is amazing
I love Fleet Foxes!! Here is a hidden gem for you, a cover of a very old song from Joan Baez all about telling a man to fuck off.

dailysoundtracks.album: The Legend of Zelda: Majora's Mask
dailysoundtracks.description: Picture me at the piano wearing a long robe playing this over and over. I love it
I love this soundtrack so much! This is one of my favourite tracks from it. What makes it special is its chord sequence!
The credits theme from an obscure old visual novel. Great vibes.
Beautiful music from arguably the best Pokemon soundtrack. I love the harmony. It feels like music to ascend to heaven to.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Masami Ueda, Hiroshi Yamaguchi, Rei Kondoh, Akari Kaida
dailysoundtracks.description: This music plays after you purify an area and I love it. This soundtrack has traditional Japanese instruments everywhere and is super ethereal and unlike anything else really.

I heard a song from this guy on an advert and looked up what it was and found this album. On this album, this one track is particularly nice and I've often come back to it. It kinda reminds me of 'Hana Valley' from Okami.
One of the best ever karaoke songs. I love it. Catch me at Monster Ronson's on a monday belting this out.

Kendrick sampled this band in 'Bitch don't kill my vibe', but I learned that after my deep dive into scandinavian indie music back in like 2010. This is the final track from their first album. I've always loved it even though to this day I've never looked up the translation of the lyrics. It's a cover of some Danish artists called Skousen and Ingemann.
Learned about this group because Kendrick sampled this song on Section 80. They have one album and it's fucking great.

dailysoundtracks.album: Lesley Step Lightly: The Gm Recordings Plus 1974-1982
dailysoundtracks.description: Found this thanks to a cover by Melody Gardot. It's beautiful and rare.
This soundtrack just like many N64 and PS1 games of this era had this awesome house/jungle vibe. This track is one of my faves from this game I used to play as a child.
The unofficial main theme of Smash 64 and huge source of nostalgia for me. It takes me right back to beating classic mode with every character as a child in front of our TV.
I love how she never sings about sex and relationships. Her songs about so many random things. This one is about new year and even features a countdown!

dailysoundtracks.artist: Naoko Mitome, Masanobu Matsunaga, Yasuhisa Baba, Kenichi Nishimaki
dailysoundtracks.description: I just love this remix of the Balloon Fight music. It's so Nintendo Wii, and makes me just want to smile for photos while holding some juice and playing tennis and shaking hands.
One of the stages in this kooky game has this music instead of the normal music and it's such a vibe. They put a whole-ass song on the GBA with switching styles and everything. I've always loved it.
I always loved this track at the end of this iconic album. It's dirty and seedy and dramatic. Who is Penelope? I bet she's up to no good.

This soundtrack is so fucking good and so nostalgic that I made a whole mix of it (available for download on this website) and have regularly listened to it in the gym. I have a secret youtube channel called Darkmooncaverns19 inspired by how good this music is.
Together with their hit Last Christmas, Wham! release 'Everything She Wants', a song with (in my opinion) questionable lyrics complaining
This is the best Christmas song and you cannot convince me otherwise.
A local band I loved growing up. This is one of my favourite songs from them, all about unplugging yourself and touching grass.
I've been a fan forever and this was the first song of hers that I heard. This is a lovely recent performance with a live orchestra. What can I say I'm just a fan and I love her.

dailysoundtracks.album: Donkey Kong Country 2: Diddy's Kong Quest
dailysoundtracks.description: When you make a game about monkeys going through the jungle you don't expect the composer to make the biggest vibes ever and some of the most iconic video game music of all time and yet here we are. Catch me listening to Stickerbush Symphony on the bus, at work, at parties and events. It's timeless.

There are a thousand 10/10 tracks to pick from on this soundtrack but I want to highlight the credits music. There's this smooth saxophone part a couple of minutes in that I love so much. They brought back David Wise one time for this soundtrack and that's why it's legendary. At the tail end there is a remix of Stickerbush Symphony. So good.
Huge banger from one of the best soundtracks ever. I literally DJ'd this track as is once because it's that good. This whole soundtrack is house bangers and I'm obsessed.

dailysoundtracks.album: Super Smash Bros for Wii U
dailysoundtracks.description: Hearing this for the first time was what made me drop everything and deep dive into everything Earthbound related. It also kickstarted my first ever video game mix back in 2014. Yoko Shimamura has gone on to become one of my favourite VGM composers.

I love this band and when I heard the first notes for the first time I immediately fell in love with this new direction they took. It sounds like Sega Genesis music! And those synth solos over those chord sequences are just amazing. Also the lyrics are so real.
The best song from Undertale. A huge track. Having explored all the previous areas with very peaceful music, this kinda of urgent disco music is such a contrast.
A noticeable shift in vibe from previous installments in this series. Gorgeous track for a serious, haunting location
A rare indie game from my childhood. This is the music from the first level and it's by far the best.
The album that this song comes from has such a special silky smooth vibe, everything reverberated and spacey. I really like this song in particular for its big climax at the end.

dailysoundtracks.track: You're Gonna Make Me Lonesome When You Go
dailysoundtracks.description: Lovely track that my mum likes. My favourite part is the second verse when this warm synth comes in. It gives me goosebumps.

This is taken from a orchestrated remix album of tracks from the original Mother on the NES. It's wild that it exists, but it's ultimately the reason why composer Shogo Sakai went on to arrange songs like Pollyanna and Bein' Friends in Super Smash Bros Melee. This album is why those tracks are so iconic. Anyway, this track is a deep cut from that album but is randomly so gorgeous. I come back to it a lot.
It's hard to pin down what genre this music is but it's so chill and beautiful. I love this track.
Some legendary Japanese electronic music! Super fun chords and synths. It sounds like video games!
Beautiful song that I've enjoyed for years and years. This song got me into Patrick Watson way back when.
Beautiful piano track from the original Hollow Knight. Stunning and easy to learn on the piano!
I'm embarrassed to talk about Muse nowadays but when I was younger I was listening to them! This track is such great rock music.
I saw this artist at a music festival many years ago and bought the CD. The final track is the one that stayed with me this whole time.
A nice low-key, kinda ominous track from this band I really like. It features Lykke Li who I also really like.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Yasuhisa Baba, Chika Sekigawa, Naoko Mitome
dailysoundtracks.description: I love this soundtrack so much. This is a super mellow track considering it's played in a cave full of mind-controlling flower people.

Underrated banger from a very easy boss in Mario Galaxy. In the game, you beat the boss before you hear the whole track, but that didn't stop the team at Mario Kart World remixing it, and it's sooooo gooooood!

dailysoundtracks.track: I Never Thought I'd See The Day
dailysoundtracks.description: A song for when you are on your own and feeling emotional. Music for gazing at the moon to. Maybe music for when something really bad has just happened.
One of the very few German-language songs I tolerate! A banger!

Please take a moment to enjoy this music video! This artist is wild and I am a huge fan. A quote from a commenter: 'she's like a witch who has spent an inordinate number of lifetimes studying the arcane arts and has forgotten what it's like to be human but woke up in a very good mood today'
Bossa nova bliss! No video game has nailed beach music as well as this. It's divine, and it's perfect.

dailysoundtracks.album: And In The Darkness, Hearts Aglow
dailysoundtracks.track: God Turn Me Into a Flower
dailysoundtracks.description: Beautiful, stunning song. Music reserved for when I'm feeling lost, devastated, wistful, broken after a major life change. The video makes me feel a lot of things.

dailysoundtracks.artist: Manaka Kataoka, Maasa Miyoshi, Masato Ohashi, Tsukasa Usui
dailysoundtracks.album: The Legend of Zelda: Tears of the Kingdom
dailysoundtracks.description: So much to love from this soundtrack, but this is my favourite. It's sparse but still has a lot going on. So pleasant to listen to
I love Norah Jones! This song however is very different from your normal Norah programming. It's a song about murdering Miriam

An English band I've followed since forever, I've enjoyed their folky music from all their albums. This is a deep cut which I love for its chill vibes. Feels like I'm at a pub hanging out with the band

I love Pikmin and this is the best music from all the games. It's a never-heard-before chord sequence over a dreamy 5/4 time signature experience. Close your eyes and enjoy the visuals as you listen to this track

Today's obscure-ass video game music comes from much-hated Fire Emblem Fates, a game that I love. It's from the later maps of the not-very-good Revelations route and it is so creepy and nice. A healthy 7/8 time signature helps too.
I'm a diehard fan of this guy and I will recommend many songs from him but here's a newer one which touched me. Nice video too
I love Michael Kiwanuka's albums but one of my favourite tracks (and videos!) is this special single track. It's rare! Take a moment to appreciate the video today, it's gorgeous.
Little Simz is so cool! She's my favourite rapper, and the music video is great.
This soundtrack didn't have all that much to offer, but I loved this remix of Super Metroid's Lower Brinstar. It's peaceful but unsettling

I can't remember how I got into this album but it's a recreation (or maybe entirely new tracks?) for the old Kojima game Policenauts. I originally heard it in a Sheik combo video. It's full of Jazz fusion bangers!
